name: Sugar and Spice Salmon
recipeInstructions:
1. At least 2 hours and up to 6 hours before you plan to smoke the fish, combine marinade ingredients in a bowl.
2. Place salmon in a plastic bag or shallow dish, pour marinade over it, and refrigerate it for at least 1 hour.
3. Bring your smoker to its appropriate cooking temperature.
4. Remove salmon from refrigerator and let it sit at room temperature for 30 minutes. Drain salmon, reserving marinade. Leave any clinging spices on the surface of the salmon.
5. Transfer salmon to smoker, skin-side down, and drizzle marinade (with some of the whole spices) generously over fish. Smoke until just cooked through and flaky, about 45 to 55 minutes at a temperature of 225 to 250°F.
6. Have a large spatula and platter ready when you take the salmon off the smoker, since it will be fragile when done.
7. Transfer salmon to the serving platter. Serve hot or chilled with some of the whole spices clinging to the fish.
Bruising technique: Bruising spices means to almost (but not quite) crush the whole spice seeds or pods by pressing on them with a pestle or the side of a knife, which releases natural oils and enhances flavor.
